name: The Trader-Thief
concept: You move the goods the houses cannot touch openly, and lift what isn't nailed down along the way. A house name at your back opens gates in every city. It also means a house owns a piece of you.
duty: Everything is for sale, including you, and the trick is never letting anyone learn your price.

look.label: Dress
options: a house sash worn or hidden

look.label: Hands
options: kept in sight to seem honest

gear.label: Stake
options: a ledger of who owes whom

moves.name: Sleight & Smuggle
moves.trigger: When you move something past someone who would stop it — goods, a blade, or yourself.

results.success: It gets through, unseen or unquestioned.
results.failure: You are caught in the act, or forced to abandon the thing to get clear.
advantage: Hold 1 Angle (spend: a hidden pocket held just what you need / a guard here already owes you / you palmed something extra on the way / you have a clean exit ready).
You learn who really controls this gate, dock, or market.
threat: Add 1 Heat: someone marks your face.
It costs a bribe, a favor, or a piece of the goods to get clear.
results.triumph: You move something huge, and no one will ever know it was you.
results.despair: Caught red-handed by exactly the wrong person.

hold.name: Angles
spend: A hidden pocket or cache holds just what you need.
Someone nearby already owes you and will look the other way.
You palmed something useful on the way through.
You have a clean exit prepared.

moves.name: House Name
moves.trigger: When you invoke your merchant house for goods, credit, passage, or muscle (Invoke Patron, core.md):
moves.text: The house answers where it trades, and adds 1 Debt. The house always collects, and it does not forgive being embarrassed.

moves.name: Appraise
moves.trigger: When you weigh a person, a good, or a deal for what it is truly worth.

results.success: You know its real value, and to whom it is worth the most.
results.failure: You misjudge it, and someone profits from your mistake.
advantage: You spot the flaw, the fake, or the hidden owner.
You know exactly what price will move them.
threat: Your interest tips someone off that this is valuable.
results.triumph: You see the whole web of who wants this and why.
results.despair: You badly overvalue a thing, and stake something real on it.

moves.name: Haggle
moves.trigger: When you drive a bargain with someone who has what you want.

results.success: You reach a deal you can live with.
results.failure: There is no deal, or the price is one you will regret.
advantage: A favor or a piece of news thrown in.
A standing arrangement for next time.
results.triumph: You get everything you wanted and their goodwill besides.
results.despair: The deal is a trap you just walked into.

moves.name: Fast Hands
moves.trigger: When you pick a lock or a pocket, slip a binding, or make something vanish.

results.success: You do it, clean and quick.
results.failure: You fumble it, and someone notices.
advantage: You get a second small thing while you're at it.
threat: You leave a trace that points back to you.
It takes longer than you have.
results.triumph: You do it under their nose and they thank you for it.
results.despair: A hand closes on your wrist.

moves.name: Contacts
moves.trigger: When you arrive somewhere the house has ever done business:
moves.text: Name a contact you have here — a fence, a guide, a corrupt official, an old flame — and take a boost die dealing with them. The GM says what has changed since you last leaned on them.

bonds: ____ has been my partner on jobs the house would never sanction.
I sold ____ out once to save my own skin, and they don't know it was me.
____ keeps my books honest, or honest enough, and I trust no one else with them.
____ thinks I can get the Legion the supplies it needs, and I've let them believe it.
I owe ____ a debt I cannot pay in coin.
communityTie: You feed the Free Legion through channels only you can reach; ____ knows the house takes a cut.
The Legion tolerates your thieving because it eats; ____ has warned you what happens the day it doesn't.
Your house wants the Legion as a customer or a mark; ____ is the one you'd never let them touch.

tracks.name: Debt
tracks.note: At 6 the house makes a major demand, then resets to 3.

tracks.name: Heat
tracks.note: Attention from marks, rivals, and the watch (core.md).

basic: Take a move from another playbook.
Take a move from another playbook.
Increase your maximum held Angles by 1.
Build a route: name a smuggling road that is always open to you between two places.
Train a move you lean on: it upgrades 2 dice to proficiency (never more than its stat).
Buy down your Debt by 1, permanently, with a clever score.
advanced: Raise one stat by 1 (max 5). Rare, and only at a true turning point.
Go independent: cut ties with the house, resolving the fallout in play, and set up on your own.
Found a trade-post: claim a Bastion facility (a market) for the Free Legion.
